children.text: A 15% tip is expected by your server for good service and up to 20% if the service is exceptional, similar to the practices in the United States. If a service charge (propina) is added to the bill (usually 10%), then a tip/gratuity is not required to be given.
10-20 pesos with every drink OR 15% for the whole bill if you are keeping an open tab.

- Porters: 10-20 per bag carried.
- Housekeeping: 10-20 pesos per day.
- Concierge: A tip of 50-100 pesos is a fair tip to a concierge who has served you well.
No tip necessary. Rounding up to the nearest 10 peso is more than sufficient.

Tipping (la propina) is part and parcel of life in the Mexican service industry. Wages of hospitality staff in Mexico is generally very low and many of them supplement their daily wages with tips. you will encounter situations where you have to tip all the time, for example like when a carpark warden helps you guide your car into a car park, or when an attendant in the bathroom helps you with a towel to dry your hands. Remember to keep small change available for these 10 peso tips because small change isn't always easy to find in Mexico.
